passage.title: Tree Season Cards
passage.text: Our group keeps a leaf color chart by the plant shelf. During science time, Noah looks at it before writing time. The leaf color chart has picture cards for each tree season for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to match the season card to the tree. Sometimes we compare today's season card with yesterday's season card and notice one small change. We like the leaf color chart because it helps us notice small changes in nature.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the leaf color chart is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the leaf color chart?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the leaf color chart is by the plant shelf.

questions.prompt: When does Noah use the leaf color chart?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Noah uses it during science time.

questions.prompt: What does the leaf color chart have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the leaf color chart has season cards.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
